iterations.theme: The Indie Developer Journey: Failures, Strategy, and Viral Success
iterations.reason_for_cut: This iteration covers the entire podcast, providing a cohesive narrative from the introduction of the guest and his philosophy to the concluding remarks on indie development.

chapters.topic: Introduction to David Smith and his philosophy of learning from failures
Podcast and guest introduction: David Smith's background and successful apps
Early days of App Store and the genesis of the Audiobooks app
Audiobooks app as a foundational success enabling full-time indie development
Inspiration and early success of the Audiobooks app
Evolution of Audiobooks monetization and the '56 failures' principle
App naming strategy and ASO in the early App Store
Changes in App Store discovery and organic downloads over time
Key lessons from app failures: market size and understanding ongoing costs
The solo developer's strategic advantage: speed and leveraging new APIs
Diminishing impact of Apple featuring for app discovery
David Smith's design philosophy: function over form and self-sufficiency
Custom iOS version analytics and community sharing
The viral success of Widgetsmith and its durability
Widgetsmith's monetization strategy: permissive freemium and covering real costs
Concluding thoughts on indie development and passion for building
